title: Meta auto-shifts spend when creative degrades
insight: When a creative starts degrading in performance, Meta's algorithm immediately begins shifting spend to the second-best performing creative in the ad set. This happens automatically without manual intervention, so advertisers don't need to pause degraded creatives proactively — the system reallocates budget on its own.
raw_excerpt: If a creative starts degrading, Meta starts immediately spending on the second best creative.

title: Avoid re-testing degraded creatives — explore new angles first
insight: Do not re-test creatives that have degraded in performance. Exhaust all other creative options and angles first. If you absolutely must re-test a degraded creative, wait 3-6 months before doing so. Prioritize testing entirely different creative angles over recycling old ones.
raw_excerpt: We do not re-test creatives that degrade - only in extreme cases after all other options and angles are depleted. If you need to do it, do it after 3-6 months ideally. But try to test different angles before this.
actionable_steps: When a creative degrades, do not re-test it — let Meta shift spend automatically
Test different creative angles and concepts instead of recycling degraded ones
Only re-test a degraded creative as a last resort after exhausting all other angles
If re-testing, wait at least 3-6 months before reintroducing the creative
